---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
editor: visual
---

List of Base Urls for county property data

```{r}
urls <- list(
  ramsey   = list(url = "https://maps.co.ramsey.mn.us/arcgis/rest/services/MapRamsey/MapRamseyOperational_AttributedParcel/MapServer/dynamicLayer/query", layer = 4),
  aitkin   = list(url = "https://gisweb.co.aitkin.mn.us/arcgis/rest/services/ParcelTaxData/MapServer/0/query", layer = -1),
  anoka    = list(url = "https://gisservices.co.anoka.mn.us/anoka_gis/rest/services/Parcel_WAB/MapServer/dynamicLayer/query", layer = 4),
  becker   = list(url = "https://gis-server.co.becker.mn.us/arcgis/rest/services/BECKER_INTERNAL_101/MapServer/33/query", layer = -1),
  beltrami = list(url = "https://arcgis.co.beltrami.mn.us/arcgis/rest/services/BeltramiBasemap2/MapServer/8/query", layer = -1),
  benton   = list(url = "https://services.arcgis.com/cHtpFLI4WlqULV8k/arcgis/rest/services/Benton_Co_Data/FeatureServer/0/query", layer = -1),
  bigstone = list(url = "https://gis.bigstonecounty.gov/arcgis/rest/services/Assessor/TaxParcels/MapServer/0/query", layer = -1),
  carlton  = list(url = "https://gis.co.carlton.mn.us/arcgis/rest/services/AGOL/Mobile_Parcel_Viewer/MapServer/dynamicLayer/query", layer = 0),
  carver = list(url = "https://gis.co.carver.mn.us/arcgis_ea/rest/services/Specialty/CC_Public_Parcel_App_Test/MapServer/33/query", layer = -1),
  chippewa = list(url = "https://gis.chippewa.mn/arcgis/rest/services/Assessor/TaxParcels/FeatureServer/0/query", layer = -1), 
  chisago = list(url = "https://gis.chisagocountymn.gov/arcgis/rest/services/AssessmentInformation/TaxParcels/MapServer/0/query", layer = -1),
  clay = list(url = "https://map.claycountymn.gov/arcgis/rest/services/CLAY_LINK/MapServer/3/query", layer = -1),
  clearwater = list(url = "https://map.co.clearwater.mn.us/arcgis/rest/services/Clearwater/ClearwaterAll_LINK/MapServer/4/query", layer = -1),
  
)
```

```{r}
library(httr)
library(jsonlite)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(stringr)

# ArcGIS includes Null for fields that don't exist, coerce those to NA to work in a tibble
nullToNA <- function(x) {
  if (is.null(x)) return(NA)
  x
}

# The JSON that is returned from the website is super messy and kept giving errors, this is to convert it all to the same format to reduce errors. Not all of this is strictly necessary as I kept adding things until it worked but it works now so I am not going to change it. 
normalizeAttributes <- function(items) {
  # Return NULL if the JSON object doesn't exist or is NULL in any way
  if (is.null(items) || length(items) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Converts messy JSON to a cleaner format: removing names, converting NULL values to NAs, flattening, and cooercing to characters anything that isn't NA
  scalarize <- function(x) {
    if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0) {
      return(NA_character_)
    }

    if (is.atomic(x) && length(x) == 1) {
      return(as.character(x))
    }

    if (is.atomic(x) && length(x) > 1) {
      return(toJSON(unname(x), auto_unbox = TRUE))
    }

    return(toJSON(x, auto_unbox = TRUE, null = "null"))
  }

  # Apply the scalarize function to each of the nested JSON items in the items array
  out <- lapply(items, scalarize)

  # Convert the flattened and cleaned JSON to a tibble  
  as.data.frame(out, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)
}

# Gets all of the IDS in the requested layer (all of the different properties)
getObjectIDs <- function(dynamicUrl, layerJson) {
  # Sends a request to the arcgis API for every ID from layer 4 and returns as a JSON object
  response <- POST(
    dynamicUrl,
    body = list(
      layer = layerJson, 
      where = "1=1",
      returnIdsOnly = "true",
      f = "json"
    ),
    encode = "form"
  )
  
# Wait until the server gets a response and continues if it is a success or returns an R error if there is some error in the request
  stop_for_status(response) 
  
  # Convert from JSON to an R object
  idsList <- fromJSON(content(response, "text", encoding = "UTF-8"), simplifyVector = FALSE)

  # Return a sorted array of the object IDs
  sort(as.integer(unlist(idsList$objectIds)))
}

# Fetch a chunk of object IDs (each chunk contains an array of object IDs)
fetchChunk <- function(url, chunk_id, out_fields, layerJson) {
  # Sends a request to the arcgis API to return the JSON data for all of the specified object IDs 
  body <- list(
    objectIds = paste(chunk_id, collapse = ","),
    outFields = out_fields,
    returnGeometry = "false",
    f = "json"
  )
  
  # If the URL is a dynamic url then a layer needs to be specified
  if (str_detect("dynamicLayer", url)) {
    body$layer <- layerJson
  }
  
  response <- POST(url, body = body, encode = "form")

# Wait until the server gets a response and continues if it is a success or returns an R error if there is some error in the request
  stop_for_status(response)

  # Convert from JSON to an R object
  data <- fromJSON(content(response, "text", encoding = "UTF-8"), simplifyVector = FALSE)

  # Convert the returned object to a flat tibble
  rows <- lapply(data$features, function(feature) {
    normalizeAttributes(feature$attributes)
  })
  
  # Sometimes the server returns empty objects if an object ID doesn't actually exist
  if (is.null(data$features) || length(data$features) == 0) {
    return(tibble())
  }
  
  # Filter out rows that are empty
  rows <- Filter(Negate(is.null), rows)

  # If all the rows were empty return an empty tibble
  if (length(rows) == 0) {
    return(tibble())
  }
  
  # Combine all the rows into a data frame and return it
  bind_rows(rows)
}

# Save a tibble to memory to allow for acontinuous data collection
saveChunk <- function(df, chunk_id, save_directory) {
  # File path to save
  file_path <- file.path(save_directory, sprintf("chunk_%05d.rds", chunk_id))
  
  # Save the file as a .rds file
  saveRDS(df, file_path)
  
  # Return the file path of the saved file
  file_path
}

combineChunks <- function(save_directory) {
  # Find all the files in the specified save directory
  files <- list.files(save_directory, pattern = "^chunk_[0-9]{5}\\.rds$", full.names = TRUE)
  
  # Combine the files into 1 data frame
  bind_rows(lapply(files, readRDS))
}

fetchParcels <- function(url, batch_size = 1000, out_fields = "*", save_directory, layerJson) {
  # Create a directory, showWarnings = FALSE makes it not show an error if the directory already exists and simply move on
  dir.create(save_directory, showWarnings = FALSE)

  ids <- getObjectIDs(url, layerJson)
  total_ids <- length(ids)
  
  # Split the object IDs into chunks. Arcgis allows for processing of 1000 object IDs in parallel
  chunks <- split(ids, ceiling(seq_along(ids) / batch_size)) 
  total_chunks <- length(chunks)

  # Print some information about the data set size
  message("Total parcel IDs: ", total_ids)
  message("Batch size: ", batch_size)
  message("Total chunks: ", total_chunks)

  # Find the completed chunks so that data isn't collected twice
  completed_files <- list.files(save_directory, pattern = "^chunk_[0-9]{5}\\.rds$")
  completed_ids <- as.integer(gsub("^chunk_([0-9]{5})\\.rds$", "\\1", completed_files))
  completed_ids <- completed_ids[!is.na(completed_ids)]

  # Find the completed IDs so that data isn't collected twice
  already_done_ids <- sum(vapply(
    seq_along(chunks),
    function(i) if (i %in% completed_ids) length(chunks[[i]]) else 0L,
    integer(1)
  ))

# Create a progress bar to show how much of the data has been collected (mainly to make sure it is actually working as data collection takes a long time and I want to see if something is happening and it isn't stuck with some error)
  progress_bar <- txtProgressBar(min = 0, max = total_ids, style = 3)
  setTxtProgressBar(progress_bar, already_done_ids)

  # Loop through all of the chunks in the chunks object
  for (i in seq_along(chunks)) {
    # Create the file if it doesn't exist
    chunk_file <- file.path(save_directory, sprintf("chunk_%05d.rds", i))

    # If the file does exist move along so not overwriting data
    if (file.exists(chunk_file)) {
      next
    }

    # Part of the progress bar
    message("\nProcessing chunk ", i, " of ", total_chunks)

    # Fetch the chunk
    chunk_df <- fetchChunk(url, chunks[[i]], out_fields = out_fields, layerJson)

    # If the fetch was successful and returned data save it to file
    if (!is.null(chunk_df)) {
      saveChunk(chunk_df, i, save_directory)
    }

    # Part of the progress bar
    done_ids <- sum(vapply(
      seq_along(chunks),
      function(j) {
        f <- file.path(save_directory, sprintf("chunk_%05d.rds", j))
        if (file.exists(f)) length(chunks[[j]]) else 0L
      },
      integer(1)
    ))
    
    # Part of the progress bar
    setTxtProgressBar(progress_bar, done_ids)
  }
  
  # End the progress bar
  close(progress_bar)

}

# Main function to actually download all of the parcel IDs
for (key in names(urls)) {
  
  # Construct a JSON object with the layer of interest on the arcgis map
  layerJson <- toJSON(
    list(source = list(type = "mapLayer", mapLayerId = urls[[key]][[2]])),
    auto_unbox = TRUE
  )
  
  parcels_df <- fetchParcels(
    urls[[key]][[1]],
    batch_size = 1000, # 1000 is the max value allowed by arcgis, can be changed to be lower for testing
    out_fields = "*", # Delimtiter value
    save_directory = str_c("Data/",key,"_parcel_chunks"), # Directory to save items
    layerJson
  )
}

```
